supply_chain.dependencies: Distribution through partners such as Sony and Microsoft for console products. Digital distribution through Apple App Store and Google Play Store for mobile. Publishing agreements with console manufacturers who unilaterally determine key commercial terms. Reliance on third-party storefronts and platform partners for majority of sales.
supply_chain.risks: Console manufacturers can unilaterally change manufacturing terms, delivery times, policies and approval conditions. Agreements with distributors are typically terminable on short notice. Platform partners have significant influence over products and services offered. Concentration of sales among top customers and platform partners. Dependence on third-party licensees to distribute and host games in certain countries.

regulatory_risks: User privacy and data collection and retention laws
Online safety regulations including content moderation and transparency
New laws or regulations on virtual items and virtual currency business models
Digital services taxes in foreign jurisdictions
Global minimum taxes (Pillar II) enacted by EU and other countries including Switzerland
Rating requirements and censorship in foreign countries
Pre-approval processes for games in certain countries
Data privacy, data protection, and security regulations (GDPR-type laws)

sanction_risks: Impact of U.S. sanctions on business operations
Trade restrictions and tariffs imposed by U.S. government on imported goods
Political instability and political developments in countries where EA operates
